name: Python
scope: An INTERMEDIATE Python developer, with 2-5 years experience, has a solid grasp of core programming concepts like data types, control flow, functions (including lambdas), and is adept with data structures such as lists, dictionaries, and sets. They are comfortable with object-oriented programming, able to create classes, implement inheritance, and understand polymorphism. They can manage modules and packages, use Python's standard library effectively, and handle third-party packages with tools like pip and virtual environments. Proficient in file operations and data serialization (JSON, CSV), they can interact with databases using SQL and ORMs like SQLAlchemy. They develop simple web applications using frameworks like Flask, consume and create APIs, and understand basic networking and concurrency (threading, multiprocessing). They write unit tests using unittest or pytest, debug code efficiently, and adhere to code quality standards like PEP 8 and best practices in documentation. Familiar with version control systems like Git, they collaborate using platforms like GitHub, participate in code reviews, and communicate effectively within a team. They can automate tasks with scripts, optimize code for performance, contribute to system design discussions, and improve existing systems while maintaining security awareness. Continuously learning, they stay updated with Python developments, seek feedback, and are capable of independent problem-solving on moderate-complexity projects.
long_scope:
An individual at the INTERMEDIATE proficiency level in Python is expected to have moved beyond the basics and can apply Python skills to solve real-world problems. They have a solid understanding of core concepts and can contribute meaningfully to projects. Below is a comprehensive scope of what an intermediate Python developer should be able to accomplish:

⸻

Core Programming Skills
	•	Data Types and Structures
	•	Proficient use of fundamental data types: integers, floats, strings, booleans.
	•	Advanced manipulation of data structures: lists, tuples, dictionaries, sets.
	•	Understanding of immutable vs mutable types.
	•	Control Flow
	•	Mastery of loops (for, while) and control statements (if, elif, else).
	•	Utilization of list comprehensions and generator expressions for efficient code.
	•	Functions
	•	Defining and invoking functions with positional, keyword, and default arguments.
	•	Understanding variable scope and lifetime.
	•	Use of anonymous functions (lambda).
	•	Modules and Packages
	•	Importing and organizing code using modules and packages.
	•	Familiarity with Python’s standard library modules (e.g., os, sys, datetime).
	•	Error and Exception Handling
	•	Using try, except, finally, and else blocks to handle exceptions.
	•	Raising exceptions and creating custom exception classes.

⸻

Object-Oriented Programming (OOP)
	•	Classes and Objects
	•	Creating classes with attributes and methods.
	•	Instantiating objects and accessing class members.
	•	Inheritance and Polymorphism
	•	Implementing single and multiple inheritance.
	•	Overriding methods and using super() to extend functionality.
	•	Understanding polymorphism and its application in Python.
	•	Encapsulation
	•	Using access modifiers to control access to class members.
	•	Understanding of getters and setters.

⸻

File and Data Handling
	•	File Operations
	•	Reading from and writing to text and binary files.
	•	Using context managers (with statement) for file operations.
	•	Data Serialization
	•	Working with JSON, CSV, and XML data formats.
	•	Serializing and deserializing data using json and pickle modules.
	•	Data Manipulation
	•	Using libraries like pandas and NumPy for data analysis.
	•	Performing data cleaning and transformation tasks.

⸻

Third-Party Libraries and Tools
	•	Package Management
	•	Installing and managing packages using pip.
	•	Understanding virtual environments (venv, virtualenv, conda) to manage dependencies.
	•	Popular Libraries
	•	Utilizing requests for HTTP operations.
	•	Implementing regular expressions with the re module.
	•	Basic plotting with matplotlib.

⸻

Web Development Basics
	•	Frameworks
	•	Building simple web applications using Flask.
	•	Understanding the basics of routing, templating, and handling HTTP requests.
	•	APIs
	•	Consuming RESTful APIs.
	•	Creating simple APIs to expose data or services.

⸻

Database Interaction
	•	SQL and NoSQL Databases
	•	Connecting to databases like SQLite or PostgreSQL.
	•	Performing CRUD operations using SQL queries.
	•	ORMs
	•	Using Object-Relational Mapping tools like SQLAlchemy to interact with databases.

⸻

Testing and Debugging
	•	Unit Testing
	•	Writing tests using unittest or pytest frameworks.
	•	Understanding test cases, test suites, and test runners.
	•	Debugging Techniques
	•	Using debugging tools or IDE features to step through code.
	•	Interpreting stack traces and logging output.

⸻

Version Control and Collaboration
	•	Git Proficiency
	•	Cloning repositories, committing changes, pushing, and pulling updates.
	•	Managing branches and understanding merge conflicts.
	•	Collaboration Tools
	•	Participating in code reviews using platforms like GitHub or GitLab.
	•	Documenting code changes and writing clear commit messages.

⸻

Code Quality and Best Practices
	•	PEP 8 Compliance
	•	Writing code that adheres to Python’s style guidelines for readability.
	•	Documentation
	•	Writing meaningful docstrings and inline comments.
	•	Generating documentation using tools like Sphinx.
	•	Refactoring
	•	Improving code structure and efficiency without altering external behavior.

⸻

Problem-Solving Skills
	•	Algorithmic Thinking
	•	Applying common algorithms and data structures to solve problems.
	•	Optimizing code for performance and scalability.
	•	Project Contributions
	•	Breaking down tasks into manageable components.
	•	Estimating time and resources required for development tasks.

⸻

Networking and Concurrency
	•	Basic Networking
	•	Understanding sockets and basic network communication.
	•	Working with higher-level network protocols.
	•	Concurrency
	•	Implementing threading and multiprocessing for concurrent execution.
	•	Understanding the Global Interpreter Lock (GIL) and its implications.

⸻

Automation and Scripting
	•	Task Automation
	•	Writing scripts to automate routine tasks (file management, data parsing).
	•	System Interaction
	•	Using Python to interact with the operating system and execute shell commands.

⸻

Security Awareness
	•	Secure Coding Practices
	•	Understanding common security vulnerabilities (e.g., injection attacks).
	•	Handling sensitive data securely (e.g., using environment variables).

⸻

Soft Skills and Teamwork
	•	Communication
	•	Effectively communicating technical concepts to team members.
	•	Continuous Learning
	•	Keeping up-to-date with the latest Python developments and libraries.
	•	Seeking feedback and actively improving coding skills.

⸻

Expected Tasks and Outcomes
	•	Develop Moderate-Complexity Applications
	•	Building applications that integrate multiple components (e.g., data processing pipeline).
	•	Maintain and Improve Existing Systems
	•	Debugging and fixing issues in existing codebases.
	•	Enhancing features and optimizing performance.
	•	Contribute to Design Discussions
	•	Participating in planning and architectural meetings.
	•	Providing input on technology choices and implementation strategies.

⸻

Tools and Technologies
	•	IDEs and Editors
	•	Proficient with tools like PyCharm, Visual Studio Code, or Sublime Text.
	•	Version Control Systems
	•	Comfortable using Git and platforms like GitHub.
	•	Databases
	•	Experience with relational databases (SQLite, PostgreSQL).
	•	Testing Frameworks
	•	Familiarity with unittest and pytest.
	•	Containers and Virtualization
	•	Basic understanding of Docker for containerization.

⸻

Mastery Over Concepts
	•	Efficient Coding
	•	Writing optimized and efficient code using appropriate data structures and algorithms.
	•	Modular Design
	•	Organizing code into reusable modules and packages.
	•	Error Handling
	•	Anticipating potential errors and writing robust code to handle exceptions gracefully.

⸻

Technical Expertise
	•	Independent Problem Solving
	•	Tackling assignments without constant supervision.
	•	Debugging Proficiency
	•	Quickly identifying and resolving code issues.
	•	Best Practices Application
	•	Implementing industry-standard practices in coding and development workflows.

⸻

Designing Systems and Processes
	•	System Design
	•	Contributing to the design of applications and systems.
	•	Process Improvement
	•	Suggesting and implementing improvements to development processes and workflows.

⸻

An intermediate Python developer is a valuable team member capable of handling a variety of programming tasks. They are not only able to write functional code but also contribute to the overall development process through effective communication, adherence to best practices, and a commitment to continuous learning.

name: MongoDB
scope: A person with INTERMEDIATE proficiency level in MongoDB as a competency should be able to design production-grade schemas using embedded vs referenced docs, polymorphic, bucket, subset, time-series and extended-reference patterns, apply JSON-Schema validation and respect 16 MB, nesting, field and index limits while balancing read/write trade-offs. They fluently write CRUD and aggregation pipelines ($match, $project, $group, $bucket, $facet, $lookup, $unionWith, $graphLookup, $reduce, $setWindowFields), leverage update operators and arrayFilters, optimise with explain plans, covered queries, early $match, range/seek pagination, $text, Atlas Search and 2dsphere geospatial queries, and manage tailable, change-stream and awaitData cursors. They craft and tune compound, multikey, hashed, partial, sparse, TTL, hidden and reshard-compatible indexes, apply hinting, analyse indexUsageStats, profiler, serverStatus, FTDC and WiredTiger cache/compression/journaling settings. They run multi-document transactions with proper readConcern/writeConcern, retryable reads/writes, idempotency, session lifetime controls and understand lock hierarchy. They deploy and administer replica sets with primary/secondary/arbiter/hidden/delayed members, configure priorities, chained replication, tag-aware read preferences, perform rolling upgrades, step-downs and election/rollback drills. They operate sharded clusters (config servers, mongos routers, shard replica sets), select hashed/ranged/zone shard keys, monitor balancer, autosplit, jumbo chunks, and execute reshardCollection with minimal downtime. Security responsibilities cover SCRAM-SHA-256, LDAP proxy, x.509, TLS, VPC/IP whitelisting, custom RBAC roles, auditing, log redaction, FLE deterministic/random encryption and FIPS mode. They plan, automate and test backups via mongodump/restore, filesystem snapshots, Ops Manager, Atlas PITR, delayed secondaries, meeting RPO/RTO targets. Tooling mastery spans Compass, mongosh, legacy shell, Atlas UI/CLI, Ops/Cloud Manager, Terraform, Kubernetes Operator, Ansible, Helm, Docker, ODMs (Mongoose, Spring Data, PyMongo/Motor, C# LINQ, Go), Kafka & Spark Connectors, BI Connector, Realm Device Sync and Atlas Search. They automate index builds, schema migrations (mongomigrate, migrate-mongoose, Mongock), seed data, blue/green or canary upgrades, FCV toggling, and embed liveness/readiness probes in CI/CD. Observability includes Atlas/Ops dashboards, Prometheus-Grafana, threshold/percentile alerts, log parsing, FTDC capture, profiler/system.profile slow-query analysis and troubleshooting of chunk migrations, NotPrimary, DuplicateKey, WT cache and step-down issues. They execute data mobility via mongoimport/export, bsondump, AWS DMS, mongomirror, Atlas Live Migrate, Change Streams CDC, pipelines to Hadoop/Spark, Kafka, S3/GCS, maintain governance artefacts, runbooks, naming/index/backup policies and mentor juniors on best practices.
long_scope:
COMPETENCY: MongoDB  
PROFICIENCY TIER: INTERMEDIATE (≈ 3-5 years focused experience)

SCOPE DEFINITION  
An individual operating at INTERMEDIATE proficiency is expected to deliver production-grade solutions with MongoDB for small-to-mid-sized systems or act as the primary MongoDB engineer for an application team inside a larger organisation. The scope below captures the full breadth and depth that must already be internalised—concepts, tasks, outcomes, tools and thinking models—before one can progress toward an ADVANCED tier.

1. Data-Modelling & Schema Governance  
• Apply non-relational modelling principles: one-to-one, one-to-many, many-to-many using embedded documents vs. references, polymorphic schemas, subset documents, bucket patterns, time-series pattern, extended reference pattern.  
• Evaluate modelling trade-offs (read/write ratio, document growth, atomicity, working-set size, index size, update frequency).  
• Enforce schema standards with JSON Schema validation, “strict mode” in ODMs, application-side contracts and versioned migrations.  
• Control document growth (padding, pre-allocation strategies, bucketing, sub-document capping).  
• Understand inherent limitations: 16 MB document size, 100-level nesting, 1,000 field limit (1.2 until 6.x), index key size, index count per collection.  
• Conduct iterative, test-driven data-model reviews using sample workloads and performance benchmarks.

2. Query Language, Aggregation & CRUD Semantics  
• Mastery of CRUD commands, update operators ($set, $unset, $inc, $push, $pull, $addToSet, arrayFilters).  
• Construct multi-stage Aggregation Pipelines using $match, $project, $group, $bucket, $facet, $lookup, $unionWith, $graphLookup, $reduce, $arrayToObject, expressions ($cond, $switch, $map, $filter).  
• Employ pipeline optimisations: early $match, covered queries, $addFields performance impact, index hand-off.  
• Pagination patterns: range-based (bookmark/seek) vs. skip/limit, “anchor document” technique.  
• Full-text search basics with Atlas Search or classic $text operator.  
• Geospatial queries: 2d & 2dsphere indexes, $near, GeoJSON types, bounding-box queries.  
• Time-series APIs (≥ 5.0): measurement vs. meta fields, bucketing config, compression, window functions ($setWindowFields).  
• Explain plans: COLLSCAN, IXSCAN, FETCH operators, executionStats vs. allPlansExecution verbosity, SBE (“Slot-Based Execution”) engine behaviour.  
• Handle cursor lifecycles, batch sizes, tailable cursors, change streams cursors, awaitData semantics.

3. Indexing & Performance Tuning  
• Design compound indexes, multikey indexes, hashed indexes, partial indexes, sparse indexes, TTL indexes.  
• Choose index ordering and cardinality based on query patterns; understand prefix rule, equality-sort-range heuristics.  
• Measure index efficiency using indexUsageStats, $indexStats pipeline stage.  
• Manage index build methodologies: foreground, background, hidden indexes, resharding-compatible indexes.  
• Apply write concern vs. journaling latency optimisation; tune WiredTiger cache size, compression options (snappy, zstd, none) and collection/ index level settings.  
• Employ serverStatus, profiler, FTDC diagnostic data and interpret key metrics (opcounters, lockedIdleReaders, page-faults, eviction, dirty percent).  
• Apply hinting, covered queries, projection optimisation.

4. Transactions, Concurrency & Consistency  
• Understand transaction ACID guarantees in MongoDB (multi-document, snapshot isolation, cross-shard limitations until 6.x).  
• Configure readConcern (local, majority, linearizable, snapshot) and writeConcern (w:majority, tagSets, j:true) relative to business SLAs.  
• Deadlock & transient transaction error handling, retry-ability logic, idempotent write patterns, retryable reads/writes flags.  
• Fine-tune session lifetimes; logicalSessionTimeoutMinutes.  
• Comprehend locks hierarchy: global, database, collection, metadata, document-level intent locks.

5. Replication & High Availability  
• Deploy and administer replica sets (3–7 members): roles (primary, secondary, arbiter, delayed secondary, hidden member), elections and rollback handling.  
• Configure chained replication, priority, votes, catch-up behaviour.  
• Seamless upgrades (rolling, FCV management) without service interruption.  
• Implement basic failover testing (stepDown, replSetStepDown, rs.freeze).  
• Use read preferences (primary, primaryPreferred, secondary, secondaryPreferred, nearest) and tag-aware routing.

6. Sharding & Scalability  
• Comprehend shard roles (config servers, mongos routers, shard replica sets).  
• Choose shard keys based on cardinality, monotonicity, zone-sharding requirements; evaluate hashed vs. ranged.  
• Manage balancer, chunk migrations, jumbo chunks, autosplit behaviour.  
• Execute reshardCollection (≥ 4.4) with minimal downtime.  
• Understand limitations of multi-document transactions, distributed transactions across shards.  
• Basic global deployments using Zones for geo-affinity.

7. Security & Compliance  
• Deploy SCRAM-SHA-256 authentication, LDAP proxy, x.509 certificates.  
• Configure Role-Based Access Control, custom roles, collection-level privileges.  
• Enable TLS/SSL, rotate keys, cipher suite policies.  
• Apply network exposure principles: IP whitelisting, VPC peering (Atlas), firewall rules.  
• Perform auditing (systemLog, file, BSON, syslog), redaction, FIPS mode.  
• Understand client-side Field Level Encryption (deterministic vs. random, key vault, KMS integration).

8. Backup, Restore & Disaster Recovery  
• Plan and execute backups: mongodump / mongorestore, filesystem snapshots, MongoDB Cloud/Atlas continuous backups, Ops Manager consistent backups.  
• Validate recovery via point-in-time restore, oplog rollbacks, delayed secondaries.  
• Manage logical vs. physical backups, compression, bandwidth throttling, schedule automation.  
• Handle backup of sharded clusters and FCV compatibility considerations.

9. Tooling & Ecosystem Fluency  
• MongoDB Atlas administration (projects, clusters, automated scaling, maintenance windows).  
• MongoDB Compass for schema visualisation, aggregation builders, explain plans.  
• mongosh (Node-based shell), legacy mongo shell, REPL scripting, .mongorc.js customisation.  
• ODM / Driver usage: Mongoose (Node.js), Spring Data MongoDB (Java), PyMongo/Motor (Python), C# Driver with LINQ support, Go Driver.  
• Atlas CLI, Terraform provider modules, Kubernetes Operator (Community & Enterprise).  
• Ops Manager / Cloud Manager for on-prem life-cycle management.  
• Integration adapters: Kafka Connector (source & sink), Spark Connector, BI Connector (ODBC/JDBC), Realm Device Sync fundamentals.

10. DevOps, CI/CD & Automation  
• Containerise MongoDB with best practices (initdb scripts, PVC, liveness & readiness probes).  
• Use Ansible roles, Helm charts, or Terraform modules to provision clusters and configure parameters.  
• Automate index builds, schema migrations (mongomigrate, migrate-mongoose, Mongock), seed data pipelines in CI pipelines.  
• Implement blue/green or canary cluster upgrades, feature compatibility version toggling.  
• Implement health probes and readiness checks for orchestrators.

11. Observability, Monitoring & Troubleshooting  
• Install and leverage Cloud/Atlas monitoring dashboards, Ops Manager metrics, Prometheus exporters & Grafana.  
• Set threshold-based, percentile-based and anomaly-detection alerts on key metrics (replication lag, WT cache pressure, lock ratio, CPU, I/O).  
• Perform slow query analysis using profiler levels, system.profile collection.  
• Capture FTDC diagnostic data and interpret; use mongotop, mongostat, atlas metrics, log redaction.  
• Debug common issues: chunk migration failures, writeConcernFailed, NotPrimaryNoSecondaryOk, DuplicateKey, WT cache full, step-down induced exceptions.  
• Execute log parsing with logRotate, logLevel adjustments, structured logging (≥ 5.0).

12. Data Mobility, Migration & Interoperability  
• Run bulk import/export pipelines using mongoimport/export, Streaming (mongoexport –type=json+–jsonArray), bsondump.  
• Orchestrate live migrations: AWS DMS, mongomirror, Atlas Live Migrate, legacy MMS restore.  
• Handle schema evolution scripts, versioning, rolling deployments with feature flags.  
• Design data pipelines to and from Hadoop/Spark (Connector), Kafka, RabbitMQ, S3, GCS.  
• Apply Change Streams for event sourcing, CDC integrations, micro-service choreography.  
• Interact with BI and analytics tools through BI Connector or federation with SQL engines (Presto, Trino).

13. Governance, Quality & Best Practices  
• Conduct schema & query design reviews, maintain shared reference documentation, decision logs.  
• Establish naming conventions, index lifecycle policies, backup retention policies.  
• Write clear runbooks, operational procedures for alert response, disaster recovery, upgrade paths.  
• Participate in security audits, compliance checks (SOC2, HIPAA, PCI) for MongoDB usage.  
• Mentor junior engineers, perform code reviews focusing on query optimisation and driver misuse.

DELIVERABLE COMPETENCIES / OUTCOMES  
• Design, implement, and own MongoDB collections powering business-critical features with predictable performance.  
• Ensure high availability via properly tuned replica sets; guarantee RPO/RTO objectives through validated backup strategies.  
• Provide first-line performance diagnosis and optimisation; escalate only complex kernel-level or global-scale challenges.  
• Automate cluster provisioning, upgrades and common maintenance tasks, embedding MongoDB steps into CI/CD pipelines.  
• Advise product and engineering teams on data-model changes, indexing strategy and transaction usage.  
• Maintain robust security posture conforming to organisational compliance requirements.  
• Demonstrate clear, data-backed decision-making rooted in MongoDB metrics and operational experience.

An engineer meeting every element in this scope reliably, repeatedly and with minimal external guidance qualifies solidly as INTERMEDIATE and is prepared to progress toward the broader, deeper responsibilities defined for ADVANCED proficiency.
